meta.document: Phase 5 Integration Review — AI Suggestion + MailLog + Statistics/CSV

strengths: Proxy architecture (React->Spring->Claude) correctly isolates PII and API keys from frontend
4 suggestion types are well-scoped: each has clear input/output/prompt structure
PII masking rules are explicit (name->surname, phone->last4, email->masked, address->omit)
Adoption tracking (adopted/dismissed/null tri-state) enables ROI measurement

concerns.issue: Tailscale VPN dependency creates single point of failure. If VPN is down, all AI features are unavailable.
concerns.mitigation: Add health check endpoint GET /api/ai/health that pings Claude API. Show VPN status in Statusbar. Degrade gracefully: hide AI panel if health check fails.

concerns.issue: Claude API response time is unpredictable (design says avg 1.4s). Complex prompts with 40K+ case context could take 5-10s, blocking UI.
concerns.mitigation: Use streaming response (SSE) instead of waiting for full response. Display partial results as they arrive. Show skeleton UI during loading.

concerns.issue: system_prompt_hash stored in DB for version tracking, but no mechanism to manage or roll back prompt versions.
concerns.mitigation: Create as_prompt_versions table or store full prompts in a config file versioned by git. Hash alone is not sufficient for debugging.

concerns.issue: SIMILAR_CASES type requires Spring to pre-filter top 20 candidates from DB before sending to Claude. The filtering logic (by product model) may miss relevant cross-product cases.
concerns.mitigation: Acceptable for MVP. Consider adding embedding-based similarity search in future iteration.

strengths: MailLog-first pattern (INSERT before gateway call) guarantees no send without log, per CLAUDE.md rule
3 channel adapters with Strategy pattern allows adding new channels without modifying core logic
Retry strategy (3 attempts: immediate/5min/30min) with exponential backoff is production-ready
parent_log_id chain for resend tracking is clean relational design
Alimtalk->SMS fallback with separate log entries maintains full audit trail

concerns.issue: System auto-triggers (6 types) use @TransactionalEventListener. If the event listener throws, the parent transaction may or may not roll back depending on Spring configuration. A failed SMS send on TICKET_CREATED could potentially roll back the ticket creation.
concerns.mitigation: Use @TransactionalEventListener(phase = AFTER_COMMIT) to ensure send happens after ticket is committed. If send fails, only the MailLog is affected, not the ticket.

concerns.issue: content_html column is MEDIUMTEXT (up to 16MB). Large HTML emails with inline images could bloat the table rapidly.
concerns.mitigation: Add constraint: max 500KB for content_html. Store inline images as separate attachments with URLs, not base64 in HTML.

concerns.issue: batch-resend endpoint (POST /api/mail-logs/batch-resend) creates N new rows and N gateway calls. With 100+ failed logs, this could overwhelm the SMS gateway.
concerns.mitigation: Add batch size limit (max 20 per request). Process sequentially with 100ms delay between sends. Show progress indicator.

strengths: Server-side streaming CSV (StreamingResponseBody) handles 100K+ rows without OOM
UTF-8 BOM for Korean Excel compatibility is a critical detail correctly addressed
5 CSV types cover all major data domains (tickets, costs, compensation, mail, summary)
PII masking per role in exports prevents data leakage
SXSSFWorkbook for XLSX with 100-row window is correct Apache POI pattern

concerns.issue: 14 API endpoints for statistics is the largest API surface in the project. Many endpoints overlap (e.g., /api/stats/trend vs /api/stats/cost-trend vs /api/dashboard/stats?view=trend). This creates maintenance burden and inconsistency risk.
concerns.mitigation: Consolidate into fewer endpoints with query parameters: GET /api/stats?view={view}&period={period}. Reduce from 14 to ~7 endpoints.

concerns.issue: 30-second auto-refresh polling for dashboard KPIs. With 12 users, that's 24 requests/minute to statistics endpoints. Complex aggregation queries (JOINing tickets+events+costs) could strain MySQL.
concerns.mitigation: Add server-side cache (Spring @Cacheable, TTL 30s). Dashboard API should read from cache, not recalculate on every request. Cache invalidation on ticket/cost changes.

concerns.issue: PDF generation (iText/OpenPDF) requires adding a Java library dependency. PDF layout for Korean text with tables is notoriously difficult.
concerns.mitigation: Defer PDF to post-MVP. CSV and XLSX cover 95% of use cases. If PDF is needed, consider HTML-to-PDF (Playwright/Puppeteer server-side) which handles Korean better.

concerns.issue: action_queue patterns (8 types) require complex SQL queries (e.g., zombie: no event for 14+ days, fake_close: CLOSED but no shipment). These queries scan full ticket+event tables.
concerns.mitigation: Run action queue detection as scheduled batch (every 5 min), store results in a cache/summary table. Dashboard reads from summary, not live query.

section2_data_flow.title: (2) Cross-Feature Data Flow & API Dependencies
dependency_diagram: │    │  reads from: tickets + events + costs + mail_logs      │         │
│    │  reads from: ai_logs (adoption rate for AI KPI)        │         │

cross_feature_dependencies.flow: User clicks '📧 이메일로 사용' on AI draft -> opens email compose modal with AI content pre-filled -> user reviews and clicks [발송] -> POST /api/messages/send with content from AI -> MailLog created -> EMAIL_SENT event -> if first outbound: FIRST_RESPONSE event
cross_feature_dependencies.risk: If MailLog API is down, AI draft cannot be sent. But draft remains visible for copy-paste.

cross_feature_dependencies.flow: as_ai_logs.adopted column -> GET /api/ai/stats -> Statistics dashboard AI KPI section
cross_feature_dependencies.risk: If ai_logs table is empty (no AI usage yet), stats API should return zeros, not error.

cross_feature_dependencies.flow: GET /api/mail-logs/stats aggregates as_mail_logs -> dashboard mail_stats KPI. GET /api/export/mail-logs streams as_mail_logs -> CSV file.
cross_feature_dependencies.risk: as_mail_logs with 2.6M rows (existing tbnws_send_sms_result migration). Stats query must use indexed columns (channel, status, created_at).

cross_feature_dependencies.flow: FIRST_RESPONSE event auto-generated when MailLog sends first outbound comm -> SLA summary calculates first response time from this event.
cross_feature_dependencies.risk: If FIRST_RESPONSE detection fails (ticket-lifecycle-tracking.md logic bug), SLA metrics will be inaccurate.

cross_feature_dependencies.flow: When AI panel is viewed, LLM_SUGGESTION_VIEWED event is logged to as_ticket_events -> appears in ticket timeline with slate dot.
cross_feature_dependencies.risk: Debounce (1 per session) must be implemented correctly. Without debounce, every panel toggle creates a new event.

cross_feature_dependencies.flow: MAIL_LOG CSV reads as_mail_logs. MONTHLY_SUMMARY CSV includes emailCount, smsCount, mailSuccessRate which aggregate as_mail_logs. AI adoption rate included in custom reports.
cross_feature_dependencies.risk: CSV export must not lock tables during streaming. Use READ COMMITTED isolation or read replicas.

shared_infrastructure.as_ticket_events: All 3 features write to or read from this table. EMAIL_SENT, SMS_SENT, LLM_SUGGESTION_VIEWED events. Statistics aggregates all events for KPI.
shared_infrastructure.spring_auth: All 3 features use the same JWT auth + RBAC. No additional auth mechanism needed.
shared_infrastructure.react_query: All 3 features use the same QueryClient. Invalidation rules must not conflict.

section3_priority.title: (3) Implementation Priority: MailLog → Statistics/CSV → AI Suggestion

rationale: CLAUDE.md DoD explicitly requires: '이메일 발송 시 MailLog 자동 생성 확인' — this is a Phase 5 exit criterion
ROADMAP.md Phase 5 DoD #1: '이메일 발송 시 MailLog 자동 생성' — first item listed
MailLog is a dependency for AI suggestion (RESPONSE_DRAFT -> send via MailLog)
MailLog is a dependency for Statistics (mail_stats KPI, CSV export, FIRST_RESPONSE SLA)
MailLog uses existing infrastructure (SMTP, SMS gateway) with lowest external dependency risk
6 system auto-triggers are needed by earlier phases (TICKET_CREATED notification, SHIPMENT_DISPATCHED notification)
recommended_order.effort: ~1 week (3 channel adapters + 8 API endpoints + as_mail_logs table + event linking)

rationale: Statistics dashboard (#14) is the index page ('/') — every user sees it on login
Report page (#13) is needed by TEAM_LEAD and ADMIN for monthly operations review
CSV export provides immediate business value (replaces manual Excel process)
Statistics reads from existing data (tickets, costs, events, mail_logs) — no new writes
No external dependency (no VPN, no 3rd-party API) — purely database aggregation
Chart library (Recharts) needs to be selected and integrated once, shared across pages
recommended_order.effort: ~1.5 weeks (9 dashboard sections + 5 CSV types + 14 API endpoints + report CRUD)

rationale: ROADMAP.md Phase 5 DoD #2: 'LLM 답변 제안 표시 (자동 발송 없음)' — second item
Depends on MailLog being ready (RESPONSE_DRAFT -> email send flow)
Depends on Tailscale VPN being available (external infrastructure dependency)
Highest complexity: PII masking, prompt engineering, streaming responses, adoption tracking
Highest risk: VPN downtime, Claude API latency, prompt quality tuning
Can function as standalone (AI panel works without MailLog, just can't send drafts)
12 users — low urgency. Manual diagnosis works fine without AI assistance.
recommended_order.effort: ~1.5 weeks (4 suggestion types + proxy service + PII masking + as_ai_logs + adoption UI)

why_not_ai_first: AI depends on MailLog for the RESPONSE_DRAFT -> send flow. Without MailLog, users can see AI drafts but cannot send them, making the feature feel incomplete.
AI depends on Tailscale VPN (external infra). If VPN setup is delayed, AI is blocked. MailLog and Statistics have zero external dependencies.
CLAUDE.md DoD prioritizes MailLog over AI: 'MailLog 자동 생성 확인' is listed BEFORE 'LLM 답변 제안 표시'.
Statistics/CSV provides immediate business value to TEAM_LEAD (2) and ADMIN (1) — they need monthly reports NOW. AI is a productivity enhancement, not a blocker.
parallel_opportunities: MailLog backend + Statistics frontend can be developed in parallel (different pages, no shared components)
AI prompt engineering and testing can start during MailLog/Statistics implementation (no code dependency, just prompt iteration in a notebook)
CSV export hook (useExportDownload) is reusable across all pages — build once during Statistics phase

risks.detail: as_mail_logs will grow rapidly. Existing data: tbnws_send_sms_result has 2.6M rows. With new system adding emails + alimtalk, expect 3-5M rows/year. as_ai_logs adds ~50K rows/year (500 calls/day * 100 workdays). as_ticket_events adds ~100K rows/year.
risks.impact: Query performance degradation on unindexed columns. Storage cost increase. Backup time increase.
mitigation: as_mail_logs: Partition by month (PARTITION BY RANGE on created_at). Keep 12 months online, archive older to cold storage.
as_mail_logs.raw_response (TEXT): purge after 90 days. Set to NULL via scheduled job.
as_ai_logs.raw_response (TEXT): same 90-day purge.
Add monitoring: alert when any table exceeds 5M rows.

risks.detail: AI Suggestion depends entirely on Tailscale VPN to reach local Claude API. VPN disruptions (Tailscale service outage, local server reboot, network misconfiguration) will make AI features 100% unavailable.
risks.impact: AI suggestion panel shows error state. Users lose productivity tool. No fallback.
mitigation: Health check: GET /api/ai/health every 60s from Spring. Cache result. Frontend reads cached status.
Statusbar already shows '🤖 AI 서포트 (Claude) ●'. Change to '⚠ offline' when health check fails.
Degrade gracefully: AiSuggestionPanel shows '현재 AI 서비스에 연결할 수 없습니다' instead of crashing.
Queue requests during short outages (<5min). Retry on reconnection.

risks.detail: Design specifies 500 requests/hour global limit. With 12 users, that's ~42 per user per hour. Claude API may have its own rate limits depending on plan. Token costs accumulate (input_tokens + output_tokens).
risks.impact: Users hit rate limit during peak hours. Unexpected API costs.
mitigation: Monitor token usage via as_ai_logs (input_tokens + output_tokens). Monthly cost report.
Optimize prompts: reduce system_prompt length, limit context injection to top 5 cases (not 20).
Cache identical queries: if same productModel+symptom was analyzed within 1 hour, return cached result from as_ai_logs.
Show remaining quota to user: '남은 호출: 28/30 (시간당)'

risks.detail: MailLog depends on existing admin SMTP and SMS gateways. These are shared infrastructure — other systems also use them. Outages or rate limits on shared gateways affect this platform.
risks.impact: Email/SMS sends fail. MailLog shows FAILED status. System auto-triggers don't deliver notifications.
mitigation: Retry strategy already covers transient failures (3 attempts).
For critical notifications (TICKET_CREATED, TICKET_CLOSED), add a 'notification_pending' flag on the ticket. Show in dashboard action queue.
Monitor gateway health: log success rate per hour. Alert ADMIN if success rate drops below 95%.

risks.detail: Dashboard makes 7+ API calls on page load, each running aggregation queries across tickets+events+costs+mail_logs. With 30s polling, this is continuous load.
risks.impact: Slow dashboard load (>3s). MySQL lock contention with CRUD operations.
mitigation: Server-side cache (@Cacheable, TTL 30s). Cache key includes role + scope.
Action queue: precompute in batch job (every 5min), store in as_action_queue_cache table.
Statistics API should use READ COMMITTED isolation to avoid blocking writes.
Consider materialized view or summary table for monthly aggregations.

risks.detail: TICKET_LIST CSV with 20 columns * 10K rows ≈ 2MB. MAIL_LOG CSV with 2.6M rows ≈ 500MB. Large downloads may time out on slow connections or mobile.
risks.impact: Download fails midway. User retries, doubling server load.
mitigation: For > 50K rows: show confirmation dialog with estimated file size.
Support date range filter to limit row count (required, not optional).
Consider async export: POST /api/export/start -> returns jobId -> GET /api/export/status/{jobId} -> GET /api/export/download/{jobId}.
Max export rows: CSV 500K, XLSX 100K (already specified).

improvements.current: AI suggestion and MailLog have separate send flows. AI draft goes to email compose modal, which then calls POST /api/messages/send.
improvements.proposed: Create a MessageComposer component that accepts pre-filled content (from AI, from template, or freeform). Both AI draft and manual compose use the same component. Single code path to POST /api/messages/send.
improvements.benefit: Reduces duplicate UI code. Ensures all sends go through MailLog regardless of source.

improvements.current: 14 separate API endpoints for statistics (trend, product-frequency, symptom-distribution, etc.).
improvements.proposed: Consolidate to 3 endpoints: GET /api/stats (with view param), GET /api/stats/action-queue, GET /api/reports. Frontend fetches specific views via query params.
improvements.benefit: Fewer endpoints to maintain. Easier to add new chart types without new endpoints.

improvements.current: Every AI request goes to Claude API, even if identical query was made recently.
improvements.proposed: Before calling Claude, check as_ai_logs for matching (suggestion_type + input_params_hash) within last 1 hour. If found, return cached output_content. Log as 'CACHED' (new trigger_type).
improvements.benefit: Reduces API calls by estimated 30% (same symptom analyzed by multiple engineers). Faster response.

improvements.current: as_mail_logs has no archival policy. Will grow indefinitely.
improvements.proposed: Monthly partition. Automated archive of partitions older than 12 months to a separate as_mail_logs_archive table (or compressed storage). UI shows 'load archived logs' button for older periods.
improvements.benefit: Keeps active table under 3M rows. Queries remain fast.

improvements.current: When AI draft is used as email, two separate logs are created: as_ai_logs (adopted=true, adoptedAction='used_as_email') and as_mail_logs (template_id=null, content from AI). These are not explicitly linked.
improvements.proposed: Add ai_suggestion_id column to as_mail_logs (nullable FK to as_ai_logs). When email is sent from AI draft, populate this column. This creates a direct link: AI suggestion -> MailLog -> TicketEvent chain.
improvements.benefit: Full traceability: 'this email was generated by AI suggestion #1001, edited by user, sent as mail log #50001, recorded as EMAIL_SENT event #8801'.

improvements.current: Frontend shows spinner during CSV download. No progress indication for large files.
improvements.proposed: For exports > 5000 rows, use async export pattern: POST /api/export/start returns jobId. Frontend polls GET /api/export/status/{jobId} for progress (0-100%). When complete, GET /api/export/download/{jobId}.
improvements.benefit: Better UX for large exports. User can navigate away and return. Server manages file lifecycle.

existing_tables_impacted.new_columns: none (all linkage via FK in other tables)

summary.overall_assessment: The three Phase 5 features are well-designed, technically feasible, and correctly sequenced. The primary integration point (as_ticket_events) is sound. The recommended implementation order (MailLog → Statistics → AI) follows dependency flow and risk profile.

implementation_order.step1: MailLog (1 week) — fulfills DoD #1, unblocks DEP-1/3/4
implementation_order.step2: Statistics/CSV (1.5 weeks) — fulfills dashboard, reads from MailLog, no new external deps
implementation_order.step3: AI Suggestion (1.5 weeks) — fulfills DoD #2, highest risk isolated to last

must_address_before_implementation: RISK-1: Define as_mail_logs partition strategy before first production INSERT
RISK-2: Verify Tailscale VPN uptime SLA and implement health check before AI phase
TF-CSV-1: Consolidate 14 stats API endpoints to ~7 before frontend development starts
IMP-5: Add ai_suggestion_id FK to as_mail_logs DDL before table creation
can_defer: TF-AI-2: SSE streaming for Claude responses (use full response for MVP)
TF-AI-3: Prompt version management table (hash is sufficient for MVP)
TF-CSV-3: PDF export (CSV + XLSX cover 95% of needs)
IMP-6: Async export pattern (synchronous streaming works for <100K rows)
